transform.name: Transform
transform.description: Receives and sends data, having applied some operation to the data.

POST.description: Send data and receive transformed result

view.name: View
view.description: Sends data only. A read-only endpoint.

operation.name: Operation
operation.description: Receives data only and returns an empty response.

POST.description: Send data to trigger an operation

directory.name: Directory
directory.description: Does not receive or send data itself, exists only to contain other resources.

GET.description: List directory contents (path must end with /)

store.name: Store
store.description: Receives and sends data, persisting each resource. A RESTful CRUD directory.

PUT.description: Create or update resource (no response body)

POST.description: Create or update resource (returns stored data)

store-transform.name: StoreTransform
store-transform.description: Combines Store and Transform. Store a configuration/script via PUT, then POST data to transform using that configuration.

POST.description: Transform data using the stored configuration

store-transform.example: HTML templates: PUT a template, then POST data to receive filled-in template

store-view.name: StoreView
store-view.description: Like StoreTransform but POST requires no input data (like a view).

POST.description: Execute with empty body to get result

store-operation.name: StoreOperation
store-operation.description: Like StoreTransform but POST returns an empty response.

store-directory.name: StoreDirectory
store-directory.description: Like Store but stored data files configure multiple endpoints in a subdirectory.

GET.description: Read the stored configuration or list subdirectory

store-directory.example: StoreTimer service: PUT config to /timers/timer01, then access /timers/timer01/start

_discovery.description: Discover the pattern of any API directory by listing with the 'details' flag
